title: This 22-year-old cancer survivor is running Boston for MGH
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Grace Taylor, a 22-year-old cancer survivor from Dedham, will run the Boston Marathon for Mass General Hospital.
Diagnosed with aggressive thyroid cancer at 19, Taylor was treated by a specialized team at Mass General.
Taylor credits her doctors and the AYA program for her recovery, inspiring her Boston Marathon run.

title: South Boston runner supports BMC social workers in Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Paige Beattie, 23, from South Boston, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team BMC.
Beattie aims to raise $10,000 to support Boston Medical Center's safety-net mission and its social workers.
Training for the marathon includes fundraising events like HandleBar classes and Stats Bar fundraisers.

title: Patience remains an elusive virtue for Legacy FC attack
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC lost 1-0 to San Diego Wave at Gillette Stadium on April 3.
Head coach Filipa Patão emphasized the need for patience and improved play in the final third.
Amanda Gutierres struggled to connect offensively, completing 11 of 21 passes against San Diego.

title: Melrose man charged with stealing hundreds of thousands from clients, prosecutors say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Henry Rappa Jr., 57, was charged with embezzling client funds and practicing law unlawfully in Malden District Court.
Rappa allegedly misused $660,000 from a client's trust account and continued legal practice despite suspension orders.
He was ordered to surrender his passport and bail set at $2,500, urged to avoid contacting witnesses.

title: Eugene Mirman posts message after fiery NH crash and dramatic rescue
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Eugene Mirman was rescued from a fiery car crash after his vehicle hit a Bedford Toll Plaza in New Hampshire.
Mirman, seriously injured, was pulled from the wreck by a state trooper and New Hampshire Governor's security detail.
The crash is under investigation with no charges filed; police seek information from the public.

title: NH trooper assaulted in unprovoked attack
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Jonathan G. Newcomb, 53, faces multiple charges after allegedly assaulting a NH State Trooper in Dover.
The incident occurred around 2 p.m. Thursday in a parking lot on Dover Point Road near Route 16.
Newcomb allegedly assaulted the trooper and tried to seize his gun; both were treated for minor injuries.

title: This runner ‘barely finished’ the Boston Marathon last year. Now he’s back to run again.
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Bryan Mallett, 24, of Lynnfield, is running the Boston Marathon to honor his late second mother, Sharla Sieve.
Sharla Sieve passed away in December 2022 from Stage 4 metastatic colorectal cancer and was treated at Mass General.
Mallett aims to complete all major marathons, with Boston being most significant as he runs to support Mass General.

title: NFL executives reportedly ‘expect’ Patriots to acquire A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: NFL executives expect Patriots to acquire Eagles wide receiver A.J. Brown after June 1, considering salary-cap benefits.
Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel remains non-committal, acknowledging potential roster strengthening through various player acquisitions, including A.J. Brown.
The Eagles may require a first-round draft pick for A.J. Brown; Patriots' interest persists amid roster needs.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Testament of Ann Lee' starring Amanda Seyfried is streaming on Hulu.
'28 Years Later: The Bone Temple' is now available on Netflix.
Ken Burns' documentary 'Henry David Thoreau' is streaming on PBS.

title: Caleb Durbin’s redemption, Willson Contreras’s swagger & other takeaways from Red Sox’ home-opening win
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox defeated the Padres 5-2 in their home opener, ending a five-game losing streak.
Caleb Durbin ended a 0-for-34 slump with an RBI single, gaining cheers after earlier boos.
Xander Bogaerts received a standing ovation at Fenway Park but went 0-for-4 against his former team.
